titles.text: AN OPEN_LABEL, MULTICENTER, NON_RANDOMIZED PHASE 2 STUDY OF ELRANATAMAB (PF_06863135) MONOTHERAPY IN PARTICIPANTS WITH MULTIPLE MYELOMA WHO ARE REFRACTORY TO AT LEAST ONE PROTEASOME INHIBITOR, ONE IMMUNOMODULATORY DRUG AND ONE ANTI_CD38 ANTIBODY
A Phase 2 Study of Elranatamab (PF_06863135) Monotherapy in Participants With MM Who Are Refractory to at Least One PI, One IMiD and One Anti_CD38 mAb

abbreviations.expandedText: National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events v5.0
American Society for Transplantation and Cellular Therapy

amendments.name: Amendment

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: This amendment introduced the study name and generic name, clarified vital sign monitoring and CRS premedication, updated sample collection procedures and bone marrow function criteria, permitted COVID_19 vaccines, clarified dosing holds for CRS/ICANS, added interim analysis and PRO analysis sets, and revised temporary enrollment hold criteria due to regulatory changes.

changes.name: Amendment
changes.summary: This amendment introduced the study name and generic name, clarified vital sign monitoring and CRS premedication, updated sample collection procedures and bone marrow function criteria, permitted COVID_19 vaccines, clarified dosing holds for CRS/ICANS, added interim analysis and PRO analysis sets, and revised temporary enrollment hold criteria due to regulatory changes.
changes.rationale: This amendment introduced the study name and generic name, clarified vital sign monitoring and CRS premedication, updated sample collection procedures and bone marrow function criteria, permitted COVID_19 vaccines, clarified dosing holds for CRS/ICANS, added interim analysis and PRO analysis sets, and revised temporary enrollment hold criteria due to regulatory changes.

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: This country_specific amendment for Belgium, Germany, Poland, and Spain, prepared per VHP procedure, clarified inclusion/exclusion criteria, mandated first dose hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and specified applicable local guidances.

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

amendments.summary: This country_specific amendment for the United Kingdom, prepared per MHRA, added an exclusion criterion for live attenuated vaccines, provided guidance for second dose monitoring for CRS risk, clarified hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and permitted COVID_19 vaccines.

changes.summary: This country_specific amendment for the United Kingdom, prepared per MHRA, added an exclusion criterion for live attenuated vaccines, provided guidance for second dose monitoring for CRS risk, clarified hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and permitted COVID_19 vaccines.
changes.rationale: This country_specific amendment for the United Kingdom, prepared per MHRA, added an exclusion criterion for live attenuated vaccines, provided guidance for second dose monitoring for CRS risk, clarified hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and permitted COVID_19 vaccines.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Age and Sex
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Male or female participants age ≥18 years.
A female participant is eligible to participate if she is not pregnant or breastfeeding. Refer to Appendix 4 for all reproductive criteria for male (Section 10.4.1) and female (Section 10.4.2) participants.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Compliance with study procedures
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participants who are willing and able to comply with all scheduled visits, treatment plan, laboratory tests, lifestyle considerations, and other study procedures.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior diagnosis of MM
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Prior diagnosis of MM as defined according to IMWG criteria (Rajkumar et al, 2014).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Measurable disease
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Measurable disease based on IMWG criteria as defined by at least 1 of the following:
a. Serum M_protein >0.5 g/dL by SPEP
b. Urinary M_protein excretion >200 mg/24 hours by UPEP
c. Serum immunoglobulin FLC ≥10 mg/dL (≥100 mg/L) AND abnormal serum immunoglobulin kappa to lambda FLC ratio (<0.26 or >1.65)

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Refractory to IMiD
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Refractory to at least one IMiD (as defined in Note below).
Note: Refractory is defined as having disease progression while on therapy or within 60 days of last dose in any line, regardless of response.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Refractory to anti_CD38 antibody
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Refractory to at least one anti_CD38 antibody (as defined in Note below).
Note: Refractory is defined as having disease progression while on therapy or within 60 days of last dose in any line, regardless of response.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Relapsed or refractory to last anti_MM regimen
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Relapsed or refractory to last anti_MM regimen.
Note: Refractory is defined as having disease progression while on therapy or within 60 days of last dose in any line, regardless of response.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior BCMA_directed therapy status
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Cohort A: Has not received prior BCMA_directed therapy.
Cohort B: Has received prior BCMA_directed ADC or BCMA_directed CAR T_cell therapy, either approved or investigational.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: ECOG performance status
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: ECOG performance status ≤2.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: LVEF
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: LVEF ≥40% as determined by a MUGA scan or ECHO.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate hepatic function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Adequate hepatic function characterized by the following:
a. Total bilirubin ≤2 x ULN (≤3 x ULN if documented Gilbert’s syndrome);
b. AST ≤2.5 x ULN; and
c. ALT ≤2.5 x ULN

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate renal function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Adequate renal function defined by an estimated creatinine clearance ≥30 mL/min (according to the Cockcroft Gault formula, by 24_hour urine collection for creatinine clearance, or according to local institutional standard method).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Adequate BM function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text:
Adequate BM function characterized by the following:
a. ANC ≥1.0 × 109/L (use of granulocyte_colony stimulating factors is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing);
b. Platelets ≥25 × 109/L (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing); and
c. Hemoglobin ≥8 g/dL (transfusion support is permitted if completed at least 7 days prior to planned start of dosing).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Resolved acute effects of prior therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Resolved acute effects of any prior therapy to baseline severity or CTCAE Grade ≤1.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Informed Consent
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Capable of giving signed informed consent as described in Appendix 1, which includes compliance with the requirements and restrictions listed in the ICD and in this protocol.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Smoldering MM
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Smoldering MM.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Active plasma cell leukemia
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Active plasma cell leukemia.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Amyloidosis
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Amyloidosis.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: POEMS syndrome
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: POEMS syndrome

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Stem cell transplant or active GVHD
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Stem cell transplant within 12 weeks prior to enrollment or active GVHD.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Impaired cardiovascular function or clinically significant cardiovascular diseases
Ongoing peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Ongoing Grade ≥2 peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy with prior BCMA_directed therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History of any grade peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy with prior BCMA_directed therapy (Cohort B).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of GBS or GBS variants or Grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: History of GBS or GBS variants, or history of any Grade ≥3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Active infections
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Active HBV, HCV, SARS_CoV2, HIV, or any active, uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection. Active infections must be resolved at least 14 days prior to enrollment.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Other active malignancy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Any other active malignancy within 3 years prior to enrollment, except for adequately treated basal cell or squamous cell skin cancer, or carcinoma in situ.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Other medical or psychiatric conditions
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Other surgical (including major surgery within 14 days prior to enrollment), medical or psychiatric conditions including recent (within the past year) or active suicidal ideation/behavior or laboratory abnormality that may increase the risk of study participation or, in the investigator’s judgment, make the participant inappropriate for the study.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous treatment with anti_BCMA bispecific antibody
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous treatment with an anti_BCMA bispecific antibody.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Previous administration with investigational drug
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Previous administration with an investigational drug within 30 days (or as determined by the local requirement) or 5 half_lives preceding the first dose of study intervention used in this study (whichever is longer).

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Investigator site staff or Pfizer employees directly involved in the conduct of the study, site staff otherwise supervised by the investigator, and their respective family members.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Known or suspected hypersensitivity
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Known or suspected hypersensitivity to the study intervention or any of its excipients.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Live attenuated vaccine
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Live attenuated vaccine must not be administered within 4 weeks of the first dose of study intervention.

arms.name: Elranatamab Monotherapy
arms.description: All participants receive Elranatamab monotherapy with a step_up priming regimen followed by a maintenance dose, with a potential change in dosing frequency.

objectives.name: Primary Efficacy Objective
objectives.text: To determine the efficacy of elranatamab in Cohort A and Cohort B

objectives.name: Key Secondary Efficacy Objective for Cohort A
objectives.text: To determine additional efficacy of elranatamab in Cohort A

endpoints.name: Overall Response Rate by BICR baseline EMD status
endpoints.text: ORR by BICR baseline EMD status per IMWG

endpoints.name: Progression_Free Survival by BICR and Investigator
endpoints.text: PFS by BICR and investigator per IMWG

endpoints.name: Severity of CRS and ICANS
endpoints.text: Severity of CRS and ICANS assessed according to ASTCT criteria (Lee et al, 2019b).

objectives.name: Secondary Safety and Tolerability Objective
objectives.text: To determine the safety and tolerability of elranatamab

endpoints.name: Duration of Response by BICR and Investigator
endpoints.text: DOR by BICR and investigator per IMWG

endpoints.name: Pre_ and Postdose Concentrations of Elranatamab
endpoints.text: Pre_ and postdose concentrations of elranatamab

objectives.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate the PK of elranatamab

endpoints.name: Complete Response Rate by BICR and Investigator
endpoints.text: CRR by BICR and investigator per IMWG

endpoints.name: MRD Negativity Rate
endpoints.text: MRD negativity rate (central lab) per IMWG

endpoints.name: Anti_Drug Antibodies and Neutralizing Antibodies against Elranatamab

objectives.name: Secondary Immunogenicity Objective
objectives.text: To evaluate the immunogenicity of elranatamab

endpoints.name: Adverse Events and Laboratory Abnormalities
endpoints.text: AEs and laboratory abnormalities as graded by NCI CTCAE v5.0.

objectives.name: Exploratory Biomarker Objective
objectives.text: To explore the relationship between elranatamab and the biology of the participant’s MM

endpoints.name: Measurements of Biomarkers
endpoints.text: Measurements of biomarkers (DNA, RNA, protein or defined cell types) resulting from analyses of peripheral blood, saliva and/or BM biospecimens

objectives.name: Exploratory PK/Efficacy/Safety/Biomarker Correlation Objective
objectives.text: To explore correlations between elranatamab exposure and efficacy, safety and biomarker endpoints, if data allow

endpoints.name: Selected PK, Efficacy, Safety and Biomarker Endpoints
endpoints.text: Selected PK, efficacy, safety and biomarker endpoints

objectives.name: Exploratory Patient_Reported Outcomes Objective
objectives.text: To assess the impact of elranatamab on patient_reported symptoms and functioning

endpoints.name: Healthcare Resource Use Data
endpoints.text: Hospitalizations, including length of stay, ICU admissions, transfusions, infections and outpatient visits

objectives.name: Exploratory Healthcare Resource Use Objective
objectives.text: To collect healthcare resource use data

estimands.name: Primary Efficacy Estimand for Overall Response Rate
estimands.populationSummary: Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (RRMM) participants meeting inclusion and exclusion criteria (All Treated Population (participants who received at least one dose of study intervention)) Summary measure: Proportion of participants with Overall Response Rate (ORR) and 2_sided 95% Confidence Interval.

intercurrentEvents.name: Subsequent anticancer therapy prior to achieving OR
intercurrentEvents.text: Receipt of subsequent anticancer therapy other than the study intervention prior to achieving an Overall Response.

intercurrentEvents.name: Death prior to achieving OR
intercurrentEvents.text: Death prior to achieving an Overall Response.

intercurrentEvents.name: Confirmed Disease Progression prior to achieving OR
intercurrentEvents.text: Confirmed Disease Progression prior to achieving an Overall Response.

intercurrentEvents.name: Discontinuation of study intervention or stopping disease assessments prior to achieving OR
intercurrentEvents.text: Discontinuation of study intervention or stopping disease assessments for any reason (e.g., unacceptable toxicity, withdrawal of consent, study termination) prior to achieving an Overall Response.

indications.description: Patients with Multiple Myeloma who are refractory to at least one proteasome inhibitor, one immunomodulatory drug and one anti_CD38 antibody

notes.text: This document and accompanying materials contain confidential information belonging to Pfizer. Except as otherwise agreed to in writing, by accepting or reviewing these documents, you agree to hold this information in confidence and not copy or disclose it to others (except where required by applicable law) or use it for unauthorized purposes. In the event of any actual or suspected breach of this obligation, Pfizer must be promptly notified.
This amendment incorporates all revisions to date, including amendments made at the request of country health authorities and IRBs/ECs and any protocol administrative change letter(s).

For participants who discontinue study intervention without confirmed PD: perform at EOT visit, then at least Q4W (±1 wk) until confirmed PD, withdrawal of consent, initiation of subsequent anticancer therapy, participant lost to follow_up, death or defined end of stu

studyInterventions.name: Elranatamab (PF_06863135)
studyInterventions.description: A novel bispecific antibody targeting BCMA on multiple myeloma cells and CD3 on T_cells for the treatment of relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma.

studyInterventions.name: Acetaminophen
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication for cytokine release syndrome prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Paracetamol
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication for cytokine release syndrome prophylaxis (alternative to acetaminophen).

studyInterventions.name: Diphenhydramine
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication for cytokine release syndrome prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Dexamethasone
studyInterventions.description: Required premedication for cytokine release syndrome prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Antibacterial agents
studyInterventions.description: Permitted and strongly encouraged for infection prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Anti_fungal agents
studyInterventions.description: Permitted and strongly encouraged for infection prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Antiviral agents
studyInterventions.description: Permitted and strongly encouraged for infection prophylaxis.

studyInterventions.name: Intravenous immunoglobulin
studyInterventions.description: Permitted and required if IgG level <400 mg/dL.

studyInterventions.name: Hormonal contraceptives
studyInterventions.description: Permitted for women of childbearing potential.

studyInterventions.name: Live attenuated vaccines
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited within 4 weeks of the first dose of study intervention.

studyInterventions.name: Drugs known to cause peripheral neuropathy
studyInterventions.description: Should be carefully considered and, if possible, avoided by the investigator.

studyInterventions.name: Investigational drugs (prio
studyInterventions.description: Must be washed out for 30 days or 5 half_lives (whichever is longer) prior to first dose of study intervention.
